prompt: A dark, gritty Chinese crime-thriller movie poster in a vertical one-sheet layout, with a moody neo-noir aesthetic and heavy cinematic contrast. The composition centers on a half-length portrait of a menacing middle-aged East Asian man with wavy black hair, wearing a dark textured overcoat over a black shirt, his face mostly swallowed by shadow, shown from chest up and slightly off-center to the right. His hands are visible in the lower-right area, calmly holding a string of dark wooden prayer beads, suggesting power, danger, and ritual. The background is a dim urban alley or old city street at night, layered with grunge textures, wet pavement reflections, decayed walls, and faint architectural silhouettes. In the lower center, place 1 small back-facing seated figure in a chair under a harsh spotlight, surrounded by 9 standing human silhouettes in the darkness, creating a tense interrogation or underworld confrontation scene. Use an almost monochrome palette of black, charcoal, and dirty gray, with deep blood-red accents and distressed white typography. On the left side, add 1 huge vertical column of expressive red Chinese brush-calligraphy title text, painted with rough splashes and dry-brush texture, dominating nearly the full height of the poster. Along the far left edge, add 2 vertical lines of smaller Chinese text in white and red: "我叫高启强，" and "风浪越大，鱼越贵". On the right side, add 1 vertical cast credit line in white Chinese text with a red circular separator: "张颂文 饰 高启强". In the lower-right quadrant, add 1 bold white brushstroke Chinese subtitle/title mark reading "狂飙", with the small English subtitle "THE KNOCKOUT" in red serif capitals beneath it. Add tiny faint Chinese release or tagline text along the bottom edge. The overall feeling should be oppressive, violent, prestigious, and highly designed, like a premium streaming-platform crime drama key art poster, with layered depth, dramatic shadows, textured smoke and grime, and realistic photographic detail.
